risk_if_not_implemented: Without Developer Architecture & Design, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Secure Engineering Principles
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the implementation of industry-recognized security, compliance and resilience practices in the specification, design, development, implementation and modification of Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS).
compensating_control_1.justification: Secure Engineering Principles (SEA-01) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Developer Architecture & Design (TDA-05) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Secure Software Development Practices (SSDP)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to develop applications based on Secure Software Development Practices (SSDP).
compensating_control_2.justification: Secure Software Development Practices (SSDP) (TDA-06) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Developer Architecture & Design (TDA-05) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
